2.  [Database](https://docs.oracle.com/en/database/index.html)
    
3.  [Other Databases](https://docs.oracle.com/en/database/other-databases/index.html)
    

Database Documentation
======================

Oracle provides a range of industry-leading on-premise and cloud-based solutions to meet the data management requirements from small and medium sized businesses to large global enterprises. These include the world's most popular open-source database, MySQL, as well as innovative In-Memory and NoSQL database solutions.

Oracle Blockchain Platform Enterprise Edition
---------------------------------------------

Provides the industry's most comprehensive and enterprise-grade blockchain platform to securely extend your business processes and applications.

*   [All Oracle Blockchain Platform Enterprise Edition Documentation](https://docs.oracle.com/en/database/other-databases/blockchain-enterprise/index.html)
    

Oracle NoSQL Database
---------------------

Provides multi-terabyte distributed storage for key-value pairs, with scalable throughput and great performance.

*   [All Oracle NoSQL Database Documentation](https://docs.oracle.com/en/database/other-databases/nosql-database/index.html)
    

Oracle MySQL
------------

Provides cost-effective delivery of reliable, high-performance, and scalable web and embedded applications through the world's most popular open source database.

*   [All Oracle MySQL Documentation](https://docs.oracle.com/cd/E17952_01/index.html)
    

Oracle TimesTen In-Memory Database
----------------------------------

A pure in-memory relational database that offers microsecond response time and extremely high throughput for online transaction processing (OLTP) applications. Applications connect and access the database using industry-standard interfaces. TimesTen also supports in-memory analytics and R programming.

*   [All Oracle TimesTen In-Memory Database Documentation](https://www.oracle.com/pls/topic/lookup?ctx=en/database/other-databases&id=timesten)
    

Oracle Database Mobile Server
-----------------------------

Provides secure connections of embedded devices and mobile applications to Oracle Database. Allows management applications, users, devices, and data on large deployments of mobile or remote devices.

*   [Release 12.1](https://docs.oracle.com/cd/E60418_01/index.html)
    
*   [Release 11.3](https://docs.oracle.com/cd/E48200_01/index.html)
    
*   [Release 11.2](https://docs.oracle.com/cd/E35865_01/index.html)
    
*   [Release 11.1.0](https://docs.oracle.com/cd/E22663_01/index.htm)
    
*   [Release 10.3](https://docs.oracle.com/cd/B28928_01/index.html)
    
*   [10_g_ (10.2.0)](https://docs.oracle.com/cd/B19188_01/index.htm)
    
*   [Oracle9_i_ Lite](http://www.oracle.com/technetwork/documentation/oracle9i-arch-901-096298.html)
    

Oracle Berkeley DB
------------------

Provides an open source embeddable database library, allowing developers the choice of SQL, Key/Value, XML/XQuery or Java Object storage for their data model. At its core is a fast, scalable, transactional database engine with proven reliability and availability.

*   [Berkeley DB](https://docs.oracle.com/database/bdb181/index.html)
    
*   [Berkeley DB Java Edition](https://docs.oracle.com/cd/E17277_02/html/index.html)
    
*   [Berkeley DB XML](https://docs.oracle.com/cd/E17276_01/html/toc.htm)
    

Java DB
-------

Provides a fully transactional multiuser relational pure-Java database that can be embedded in data-rich applications or deployed as a stand-alone server. Its SQL and JDBC support ensure a path to an enterprise-level database when the need arises.

*   [All Java DB Documentation](https://docs.oracle.com/javadb/index.html)
    

Oracle Rdb
----------

Provides a full-featured, relational database management system for mission-critical applications on OpenVMS platforms.

*   [All Oracle Rdb Documentation](http://www.oracle.com/technetwork/database/database-technologies/rdb/documentation/index.html)
    

Oracle Essbase
--------------

Quickly leverage and integrate data from multiple existing data sources and distribute filtered information to end-user communities in the format that best meets the users's needs.

*   [All Oracle Essbase Documentation](https://docs.oracle.com/en/database/other-databases/essbase/index.html)
    

[Back to main content](https://docs.oracle.com/en/database/other-databases/index.html#)

# REST APIs

### [Oracle Cloud Infrastructure Documentation](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/home.htm)

* * *

All Pages

[Skip to main content](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/API/Concepts/usingapi.htm#dcoc-content-body)

REST APIs
=========

The Oracle Cloud Infrastructure APIs are typical REST APIs that use HTTPS requests and responses. This topic describes basic information about using the APIs.

API Reference and Endpoints
---------------------------

For links to the Oracle Cloud Infrastructure API reference and a list of the regional API endpoints, see [API Reference and Endpoints](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/api/)
.

API Version
-----------

The base path of the endpoint includes the desired API version (for example, 20160918). Here's an example for a POST request to create a new VCN in the Ashburn region:

    
    POST https://iaas.us-ashburn-1.oraclecloud.com/20160918/vcns

API Breaking Changes Policy
---------------------------

Oracle Cloud Infrastructure provides 12 months advance notice before the date of removing or changing an existing API of a Cloud Service that you have deployed which would require you to update the code.

Request Signing Required
------------------------

All Oracle Cloud Infrastructure API requests must be signed for authentication purposes. For information about the required credentials and how to sign the requests, see [Request Signatures](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/API/Concepts/signingrequests.htm#Request_Signatures)
.

HTTPS and TLS 1.2 Required
--------------------------

All Oracle Cloud Infrastructure API requests must support HTTPS and SSL protocol TLS 1.2.

Maximum Allowed Client Clock Skew
---------------------------------

HTTP status code 401 (NotAuthenticated) is returned if the client's clock is skewed more than 5 minutes from the server's. To determine the server's clock time, use this curl command with the API endpoint:

    curl -s --head <endpoint> | grep Date

For example:

    curl -s --head https://iaas.us-phoenix-1.oraclecloud.com | grep Date

Request and Response Format
---------------------------

The Oracle Cloud Infrastructure APIs use standard HTTP requests and responses. Each may contain Oracle-specific headers for pagination, entity tags (ETags), and so on as described elsewhere in this topic and in the API documentation.

Each response includes a unique Oracle-assigned request ID (for example, bb3f3275-f356-462a-93c4-bf40fb82bb02) in the `opc-request-id` response header. If you need to contact Oracle about a particular request, please provide this request ID.

Many of the API operations require JSON in the request body or return JSON in the response body. The specific contents of the JSON are described in the API documentation for the individual operation. Notice that the JSON is not wrapped or labeled according to the operation's name or the object's name or type.

**Note**  
  

Make sure to set the `Content-Type` header to `application/json` in your POST and PUT requests that contain JSON in the body.

### Example CreateVcn Request

    
    POST https://iaas.us-phoenix-1.oraclecloud.com/20160918/vcns
    host: iaas.us-phoenix-1.oraclecloud.com
    opc-retry-token: 239787fs987
    Content-Type: application/json
    HTTP headers required for authentication
    Other HTTP request headers per the HTTP spec
    
    {
      "compartmentId": "ocid1.compartment.oc1..aaaaaaaauwjnv47knr7uuuvqar5bshnspi6xoxsfebh3vy72fi4swgrkvuvq",
      "displayName": "Apex Virtual Cloud Network",
      "cidrBlock": "172.16.0.0/16"
    }

### Example CreateVcn Response

    200 OK
    opc-request-id: 6c4d01a6-f764-4325-a3f8-720c8b5cae7b
    
    {
       "id": "ocid1.vcn.oc1.phx.aaaaaaaa4ex5pqjtkjhdb4h4gcnko7vx5uto5puj5noa5awznsqpwjt3pqyq",
       "compartmentId": "ocid1.compartment.oc1..aaaaaaaauwjnv47knr7uuuvqar5bshnspi6xoxsfebh3vy72fi4swgrkvuvq",
       "displayName": "Apex Virtual Cloud Network",
       "cidrBlock": "172.16.0.0/16"
       "defaultRouteTableId": "ocid1.routetable.oc1.phx.aaaaaaaaba3pv6wkcr4jqae5f44n2b2m2yt2j6rx32uzr4h25vqstifsfdsq",
       "defaultSecurityListId": "ocid1.securitylist.oc1.phx.aaaaaaaac6h4ckr3ncbxmvwinfvzxjbr7owu5hfzbvtu33kfe7hgscs5fjaq"
       "defaultDhcpOptionsId": "ocid1.dhcpoptions.oc1.phx.aaaaaaaawglzn7s5sogyfznl25a4vxgu76c2hrgvzcd3psn6vcx33lzmu2xa"
       "state": "PROVISIONING",
       "timeCreated": "2016-07-22T17:43:01.389+0000"
    }		

Error Format
------------

If a request results in an error, the response contains a standard HTTP response code with 4xx for client errors and 5xx for server errors. The body also includes JSON with an error code and a description of the error. For example:

    {
        "code": "InvalidParameter",
        "message": "Description may not be empty; description size must be between 1 and 400"
    }

For a list of common errors across all services, see [APIÂ Errors](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/API/References/apierrors.htm#API_Errors)
.

Request Throttling
------------------

Oracle Cloud Infrastructure applies throttling to many API requests to prevent accidental or abusive use of resources. If you make too many requests too quickly, you might see some succeed and others fail. Oracle recommends that you implement an exponential back-off, starting from a few seconds to a maximum of 60 seconds. When a request fails due to throttling, the system returns response code 429 and the following error code and description:

    {
          "code": "TooManyRequests",
          "message": "User-rate limit exceeded."
    }

Polling for Resource Status
---------------------------

Most Oracle Cloud Infrastructure resources, such as compute instances, have lifecycles. In many cases, you want your code to wait until a resource or **work request**Â  reaches a specific state, or a timeout is exceeded, before taking further action.

You can poll a resource to determine its state. For example, when you call [GetInstance](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/api/#/en/iaas/latest/Instance/GetInstance)
, the response body contains an [instance resource](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/api/#/en/iaas/latest/Instance/)
 that includes the `lifecycleState` attribute. You might want your code to wait until the instance's `lifecycleState` is RUNNING before proceeding.

Different resources take different amounts of time to transition between states. Therefore, the optimal frequency and duration parameters for a polling strategy can vary among resources. The Oracle Cloud Infrastructure SDK waiters use the following default strategy:

*   Use an exponential back-off, starting from a few seconds to a maximum of 30 seconds between poll attempts.
*   Poll up to 20 minutes, and then stop.

Or more information on waiters, see:

*   [SDK for Java waiters documentation](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/API/SDKDocs/javasdkconcepts.htm#waiters)
    
*   [SDK for Ruby waiters documentation](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/tools/ruby/latest/OCI/Response.html)
    

Where to Find Your Tenancy's OCID
---------------------------------

If you use the API, you'll need your **tenancy**Â 's OCID in order to sign the requests (see [Request Signatures](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/API/Concepts/signingrequests.htm#Request_Signatures)
). You'll also need it for some of the IAM API operations. An OCID is an Oracle Cloud ID (see [Resource Identifiers](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/General/Concepts/identifiers.htm)
).

Get the tenancy OCID from the Oracle Cloud Console on the **Tenancy Details** page:

1.  In the **navigation menu**Â , select the **Profile** menu ![Profile menu icon](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/Resources/Images/usermenu.png) and then select **Tenancy: <your\_tenancy\_name>**.
    
2.  The tenancy OCID is shown under **Tenancy Information**. Select **Show** to display the entire ID or select **Copy** to copy it to your clipboard.
    
    [![Tenancy Details page showing the location of the tenancy OCID](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/libraries/global-images/tenancy-details-page.png "Click to expand")](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/libraries/global-images/tenancy-details-page.png)
    

The tenancy OCID looks something like this (notice the word "tenancy" in it): `ocid1.tenancy.oc1..<unique_ID>`.

List Pagination
---------------

Most List operations paginate results. For example, results are paginated for the [ListInstances](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/api/#/en/iaas/latest/Instance/ListInstances)
 operation in the Core Services API. When you call a paginated List operation, the response indicates more pages of results by including the `opc-next-page` header.

**Note**  
  
A page can be empty even when more results remain. Anytime the `opc-next-page` header appears, there are more list items to get. For more information about resource list control, see [Overview of Search](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/Search/Concepts/queryoverview.htm)
.

List pagination for Object Storage [ListObjects](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/api/#/en/objectstorage/latest/Object/ListObjects)
 works differently because the pagination controls are also used for object name filtering. [ListObjects](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/api/#/en/objectstorage/latest/Object/ListObjects)
 returns `nextStartWith` instead of `opc-next-page` in the response body. To paginate through more objects, use the returned `nextStartWith` value with the `start` parameter. To filter which objects ListObjects returns, use the `start` and `end` parameters.

[To get the next page of results](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/API/Concepts/usingapi.htm#)

Make a new GET request against the same URL, modified by setting the page query parameter to the value from the `opc-next-page` header. Repeat this process until you get a response without an `opc-next-page` header. The absence of this header indicates that you have reached the last page of the list.

**Note**  
  
For an alternative to writing pagination code, see the functions in the [pagination module](https://oracle-cloud-infrastructure-python-sdk.readthedocs.io/en/latest/pagination.html)
 provided with the SDK for Python.

[To get the previous page of results](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/API/Concepts/usingapi.htm#)

(Available with some APIs.)Â Make a new GET request against the same URL, modified by setting the page query parameter to the value from the `opc-prev-page` header. Repeat this process until you get a response without an `opc-prev-page` header. The absence of this header indicates that you have reached the first page of the list.

**Note**  
  
For an alternative to writing pagination code, see the functions in the [pagination module](https://oracle-cloud-infrastructure-python-sdk.readthedocs.io/en/latest/pagination.html)
 provided with the SDK for Python.

[To change the maximum number of results per page](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/API/Concepts/usingapi.htm#)

In the GET request, set the `limit` to the number of items you want returned in the response.

**Note**  
  
The service will return no more than the number specified as `limit`, but might not return that exact number.

Retry Token
-----------

For some operations you can provide a unique retry token (`opc-retry-token`) so the request can be retried in case of a timeout or server error without the risk of executing that same action again. The token expires after 24 hours, but can be invalidated before then due to conflicting operations (for example, if a resource has been deleted and purged from the system, then a retry of the original creation request may be rejected).

ETags for Optimistic Concurrency Control
----------------------------------------

The API supports etags for the purposes of optimistic concurrency control. The GET and POST calls return an `etag` response header with a value you should store. When you later want to update or delete the resource, set the `if-match` header to the ETag you received for the resource. The resource will then be updated or deleted _only_ if the ETag you provide matches the current value of that resource's ETag.

Null vs. Empty Strings for Optional Parameters
----------------------------------------------

If you send an empty string ("") as the value of an optional parameter, the API validates the value as normal (for example, checks against minimum and maximum allowed length, and so on). Often the minimum allowed length is 1, so an error would be returned. If you don't set the value (it's null), the API performs no validation, and some other action may occur. For example: if you don't set a value for the `displayName` when creating a new VCN object, the service will auto-generate a value.

*   [REST APIs](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/API/Concepts/usingapi.htm#REST_APIs)
    
*   [API Reference and Endpoints](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/API/Concepts/usingapi.htm#one)

![](https://docs.oracle.com/en/bigdata/big-data-sql/sp_common/shared-images/12-bigdata.png)

1.  [Home](https://docs.oracle.com/index.html)
    
2.  [Big Data](https://docs.oracle.com/en/bigdata/index.html)
    
3.  [Big Data SQL](https://docs.oracle.com/en/bigdata/big-data-sql/index.html)
    

Oracle Big Data SQL
===================

Oracle Big Data SQL lets you use the full power of Oracle SQL to seamlessly access and integrate data stored across Oracle Database, Hadoop, Kafka, NoSQL, and object store sources. It extends Oracle Database security to all of your data. Its unique Smart Scan leverages the cluster to parse, intelligently filter and aggregate data where it resides. By distributing processing and minimizing data movement, Smart Scan maximizes query performance.

Big Data SQL Releases
---------------------

# Oracle GoldenGate - Oracle GoldenGate Related Products

[Go to main content](https://docs.oracle.com/en/middleware/goldengate/related-products.html#)

 Search

*   [Help Center Home](https://docs.oracle.com/)
    

*   [Middleware](https://docs.oracle.com/en/middleware/index.html)
    
*   [Oracle GoldenGate](https://docs.oracle.com/en/middleware/goldengate/index.html)
    *   [Related Products](https://docs.oracle.com/en/middleware/goldengate/related-products.html)
        

![](https://docs.oracle.com/en/middleware/goldengate/sp_common/shared-images/goldengate.png)

1.  [Home](https://docs.oracle.com/index.html)
    
2.  [Middleware](https://docs.oracle.com/en/middleware/index.html)
    
3.  [GoldenGate](https://docs.oracle.com/en/middleware/goldengate/index.html)
    

Oracle GoldenGate
=================

Looking for documentation, tutorials, or videos so you can learn more about Oracle GoldenGate products? You can find all of these resources and more here in the Oracle Help Center.

Oracle GoldenGate Related Products
----------------------------------

### [Oracle GoldenGate Plug-in for EMCC](https://docs.oracle.com/en/middleware/goldengate/emplugin/index.html)

Oracle GoldenGate Plug-in for EMCC extends Oracle Enterprise Manager Cloud Control to support for monitoring and managing Oracle GoldenGate processes. By deploying the plug-in in your Cloud Control environment, you gain the ability to interact with Oracle GoldenGate processes, configurations, log files, alerts, and user security.

### [Oracle GoldenGate HP NonStop (Guardian)](https://docs.oracle.com/en/middleware/goldengate/non-stop/index.html)

Oracle GoldenGate for HP NonStop is a comprehensive software package for enabling the replication of data in heterogeneous data environments. The product set enables high availability solutions, real-time data integration, transactional change data capture, data replication, and data manipulation.

### [Oracle GoldenGate Application Adapters](https://docs.oracle.com/en/middleware/goldengate/adapter/index.html)

Oracle GoldenGate Application Adapters allow you to integrate with Oracle GoldenGate instances to bring in Java Message Service (JMS) information or to deliver information as JMS messages or as files.

### [Oracle GoldenGate Monitor](https://docs.oracle.com/en/middleware/goldengate/monitor/index.html)

Oracle GoldenGate Monitor is a real-time, Web-based monitoring console for the Oracle GoldenGate replication solution. Oracle GoldenGate Monitor delivers an at-a-glance, graphical view of all of the Oracle GoldenGate instances and their associated databases within your enterprise.

### [Oracle GoldenGate Studio](https://docs.oracle.com/en/middleware/goldengate/studio/index.html)

With Oracle GoldenGate Studio, users new to Oracle GoldenGate can start replicating data with minimal effort while experienced users have access to GoldenGateâs advanced replication options.

### [Oracle GoldenGate Director](https://docs.oracle.com/goldengate/dir1212/gg-director/index.html)

Oracle GoldenGate Director is a multi-tiered client-server application that enables the configuration and management of Oracle GoldenGate instances from a remote client.

[Back to main content](https://docs.oracle.com/en/middleware/goldengate/related-products.html#)

[Skip Navigation](https://docs.oracle.com/cd/E60418_01/index.html#CONTENT "Skip Navigation")

*   [Overview](https://docs.oracle.com/cd/E60418_01/index.html#)
    

Database

Oracle Database Mobile Server Documentation  
Release 12.1
==========================================================

Overview
--------

### Welcome

Mobile Server enables the delivery and operation of secure, personalized applications to mobile devices. You can develop and deploy database applications for mobile environments with persistent access to critical information without requiring continuous connectivity to back-end enterprise systems.

Converged Charging System Documentation
=======================================

Oracle Communications Converged Charging System (CCS) is a cloud-native solution based on microservices architecture. You use it to enable 5G Core Network Functions (NFs) to communicate with evolved packet core (EPC) network elements.

2.  [Database](https://docs.oracle.com/en/database/index.html)
    
3.  [Other Databases](https://docs.oracle.com/en/database/other-databases/index.html)
    
4.  [TimesTen In-Memory Database](https://docs.oracle.com/en/database/other-databases/timesten/index.html)
    

Oracle TimesTen In-Memory Database
==================================

Oracle TimesTen In-Memory Database is the world's fastest OLTP database. It is a relational in-memory database with a rich feature set. TimesTen supports SQL, standard APIs, complete ACID properties, and highly available replication mechanisms. A TimesTen database resides entirely in physical memory and is persistent and recoverable.Â By managing data in memory and optimizing data structures and access algorithms, database operations run efficiently, achieving dramatic gains in responsiveness and throughput.Â 

**Select release:**

Release 22.1 Release 18.1 11g Release 2 (11.2.2)

### Highlights

**TimesTen In-Memory Database for Kubernetes**  
The TimesTen Operator enables TimesTen deployment on Oracle Cloud Infrastructure (OCI) Container Engine for Kubernetes or on on-premises infrastructure. It can quickly deploy TimesTen in standalone mode or as a cache (for the Oracle Database). To learn more, see [Kubernetes Operator User's Guide](https://www.oracle.com/pls/topic/lookup?ctx=en/database/other-databases/timesten/22.1&id=TTKUB)
.

**TimesTen In-Memory Database for Kubernetes - BYOL**  
This new application in Oracle Cloud Marketplace enables you to quickly deploy TimesTen databases in Oracle Cloud Infrastructure (OCI). To get started, see [Using Oracle Cloud Marketplace to Obtain a TimesTen Container Image (BYOL)](https://www.oracle.com/pls/topic/lookup?ctx=en/database/other-databases/timesten/22.1&id=TTMKH)

**TimesTen on Oracle Linux Arm**  
TimesTen Classic is now certified to support the popular Arm architecture. See the [Installation, Migration, and Upgrade Guide](https://www.oracle.com/pls/topic/lookup?ctx=en/database/other-databases/timesten/22.1&id=TTINS)
. You can apply the Linux/UNIX examples of this content to Oracle Linux for Arm.

Likewise, the TimesTen Kubernetes Operator now supports the arm64 platform for TimesTenClassic objects. See the [Release Notes](https://www.oracle.com/pls/topic/lookup?ctx=en/database/other-databases/timesten/22.1&id=TTREL)
 for details.

### Learn

![](https://docs.oracle.com/en/database/other-databases/timesten/sp_common/shared-images/attention.png)

  
**Upgrade To a Supported Release**  
Release 11g Release 2 (11.2.2) is an end-of-life version of TimesTen. [TimesTen 22.1](https://www.oracle.com/pls/topic/lookup?ctx=en/database/other-databases/timesten&id=timesten22.1_release_page)
 and [TimesTen 18.1](https://www.oracle.com/pls/topic/lookup?ctx=en/database/other-databases/timesten&id=timesten-18.1-indexpage)
 are currently supported long-term releases.

To upgrade to these releases:

1.  Enhancements made between releases to the replication protocol mean that to perform an online upgrade between 11.2.2.8 and 22.1 or 18.1, the 11.2.2.8 release must be 11.2.2.8.27 or later. If you are using a version prior to the 11.2.2.8.27, upgrade to a newer 11.2.2.8 release. See [TimesTen Upgrades](https://docs.oracle.com/cd/E21901_01/timesten.1122/e21632/upgrade.htm#TTINS199)
    .
2.  Move to 22.1. See [Moving to a different major release using ttMigrate](https://docs.oracle.com/en/database/other-databases/timesten/22.1/installation/upgrades-timesten-classic1.html#GUID-6CF35B6A-9A3C-4D3E-863D-26EF77534827)
    . You can alternatively migrate to 18.1. See [Offline upgrade: Moving to a different major release.](https://docs.oracle.com/database/timesten-18.1/TTINS/upgrade.htm#TTINS220)
    

### Learn

*   [Learn the features and deployment options](https://www.oracle.com/pls/topic/lookup?ctx=en/database/other-databases/timesten&id=timesten11.2.2_feat_deploy)
    
*   [Use our tutorials and demos](https://www.oracle.com/pls/topic/lookup?ctx=en/database/other-databases/timesten&id=timesten11.2.2_tutorials)
    

### Access Our Bookshelf

Oracle Identity Cloud Service
=============================

Get Started
-----------

Oracle Identity Cloud Service provides an innovative, fully integrated service that delivers all the core identity and access management capabilities through a multi-tenant Cloud platform.

After you receive your activate your account email and change your password, there are several tasks you will want to do before you start using Oracle Identity Cloud Service.

**Tell us who you are to get started:**

Administrator User Developer

Offline Mediation Controller Documentation
==========================================

Oracle Communications Offline Mediation Controller provides mediation for data created by multiservice, IP, and mobile networks. Offline Mediation Controller transforms, enriches, normalizes, and aggregates data into consistent formats that can be used by charging, billing, and analytics systems.

*   [Release 15.1](https://docs.oracle.com/en/industries/communications/offline-mediation-controller/15.1/index.html)
    
*   [Release 15.0](https://docs.oracle.com/en/industries/communications/offline-mediation-controller/15.0/index.html)
    
*   [Release 12.0](https://docs.oracle.com/en/industries/communications/offline-mediation-controller/12.0/index.html)
    
*   [Release 6.0](https://docs.oracle.com/cd/E26681_01/index.htm)
    

Network Mediation
-----------------

*   [Release 5.1](https://docs.oracle.com/cd/E14870_01/index.html)

Converged Application Server, Service Controller Documentation
==============================================================

Oracle Communications Converged Application Server, Service Controller (CAS-SC) provides real-time service orchestration and protocol mediation capabilities. With a versatile orchestration engine and portfolio of standard interfaces, CAS-SC provides a gradual migration path from legacy infrastructure to next generation platforms. It enables CSPs to launch innovative new services that blend legacy and Internet Protocol (IP) networks.

*   [Release 6.2](https://docs.oracle.com/cd/F18705_01/index.htm)
    
*   [Release 6.1](https://docs.oracle.com/cd/E39415_01/index.htm)
    

[Back to main content](https://docs.oracle.com/en/industries/communications/service-controller/index.html#)

1.  [Home](https://docs.oracle.com/index.html)
    
2.  [Industries](https://docs.oracle.com/en/industries/index.html)
    
3.  [Communications](https://docs.oracle.com/en/industries/communications/index.html)
    
4.  [Evolved Communications Application Server](https://docs.oracle.com/en/industries/communications/evolved-communication-application-server/index.html)
    

Evolved Communications Application Server Documentation
=======================================================

Oracle Communications Evolved Communications Application Server enables you to provide SIP multimedia services to your IMS subscribers. It contains a Voice over Long Term Evolution (VoLTE) and Wi-Fi-based VOIP (VoWiFi) application that you can modify and expand to define the services that you offer to your subscribers. It includes an easy-to-use GUI interface for modifying SIP call behavior and session control logic and provides drag-and-drop control over the workflow from creation through to testing, staging, and ultimately production.

*   [Release 7.1](https://docs.oracle.com/cd/E66293_01/index.htm)
    
*   [Release 7.0](https://docs.oracle.com/cd/E50752_01/index.htm)
    

[Back to main content](https://docs.oracle.com/en/industries/communications/evolved-communication-application-server/index.html#)

GlassFish Communications Server Documentation
=============================================

Oracle GlassFish Communications Server is an easy, fast, and industry-leading application server based on Java Platform, Enterprise Edition (Java EE) and SIP technology for developing and delivering web applications and web services. Use it to build SIP applications and converged applications that are JSR 289âcomplaint. It provides superior performance, clustering, and high availability features for scalable, carrier-grade services that continue to operate despite software and hardware faults.

JD Edwards EnterpriseOne Human Capital Management
=================================================

Get Started
-----------

Oracle JD Edwards EnterpriseOne Human Capital Management (HCM) solution is a suite of integrated, collaborative applications designed to help streamline HR operations by reducing time-intensive administrative tasks and lowering costs by deploying self-service applications. The Human Capital Management solution includes: Human Resources Management, Payroll, Self Service Human Resources, and Time and Labor.

Application Session Controller Documentation
============================================

Oracle Communications Application Session Controller is an integrated communications controller and web application server that enables service providers, enterprises, and contact centers to orchestrate and control real-time voice and video communications using web services. Advanced server-based software, Oracle Communications Application Session Controller enables real-time communications between business applications and communications systems based on Session Initiation Protocol (SIP).

Enterprise Performance Management Documentation Release 11.2.x

Enterprise Performance Management software helps you analyze, understand, and report on your business. Enterprise Performance Management includes a suite of performance management applications, a common foundation of tools and products, and a variety of data sources.

Oracle Big Data Connectors
==========================

Oracle Big Data Connectors is a software suite that integrates processing in Apache Hadoop distributions with operations in Oracle Database. It enables the use of Hadoop to process and analyze massive volumes of data and to use it with database data to derive new and critical business insights.  
The tools in this suite include Oracle SQL Connector for Hadoop Distributed File System, Oracle Loader for Hadoop, Oracle Data Integrator, Oracle XQuery for Hadoop, Oracle R Advanced Analytics for Hadoop, and Oracle Datasource for Apache Hadoop.

----------------------------

3.  [Communications](https://docs.oracle.com/en/industries/communications/index.html)
    
4.  [Tekelec HLR Router](https://docs.oracle.com/en/industries/communications/tekelec-hlr-router/index.html)
    

Tekelec HLR Router Documentation
================================

Oracle Communications Tekelec HLR Router allows the optimization of HLR workloads over mobile networks by providing a centralized database of subscriber to HLR mappings. This allows mobile network operators to optimize the workloads of HLRs by pairing subscribers with HLRs based on their signaling activity patterns. It also optimizes capacity for each HLR by allowing subscriber ranges to be split over different HLRs and allows individual subscribers to be assigned to any HLR.

*   [Release 4.1](https://docs.oracle.com/cd/E76437_01/index.htm)
    
*   [Release 4.0](https://docs.oracle.com/cd/E53657_01/index.htm)
    

[Back to main content](https://docs.oracle.com/en/industries/communications/tekelec-hlr-router/index.html#)

WebRTC Session Controller Documentation
=======================================

Oracle Communications WebRTC Session Controller enables real-time communication between Web browsers, between Web browsers and SIP phones, and between Web browsers and PTSN phones. WebRTC Session Controller provides an integrated application platform that developers and system administrators use to create and deploy converged WebRTC applications. For example, developers can incorporate functions such as video chats, audio communications, and data transfers into their Web applications.

*   [Release 7.2.1](https://docs.oracle.com/cd/F33078_01/index.htm)

JD Edwards EnterpriseOne Asset Lifecycle Management
===================================================

Get Started
-----------

Oracle JD Edwards EnterpriseOne Asset Lifecycle Management (ALM) solution helps drive greater value from your assets, whether they are plants, facilities, or equipment. From capital planning and budgeting, to procurement and operations, to maintenance and repair, JD Edwards EnterpriseOne ALM can maximize profitability and return on investment across the entire asset lifecycle. The Asset Lifecycle Management solution includes: Conditioned-Based Maintenance, Rental Management, Capital Asset Management, Equipment Cost Analysis, and Resource Assignments.

**Select your release:**

Release 9.2 Previous Releases

Hospitality
===========

Oracle Hospitality delivers a wide range of software, hardware, and related servicesâalong with a rapidly growing portfolio of cloud solutionsâto enable our customers in the hospitality industry to provide superior service and experience to their guests anywhere.

Integration Platforms
---------------------

Oracle Hospitality provides platform and payment card solutions that enable seamless integration between Oracle Hospitality point-of-sale and hotel solutions and third-party websites, apps, and payment transaction processors.

Billing and Revenue Management Documentation
============================================

Oracle Communications Billing and Revenue Management (BRM) provides an end-to-end revenue management system for communications service providers. Use BRM to configure product offerings, create customer accounts, charge for service usage, collect and analyze revenue, and manage customer relationships.

### Billing and Revenue Management Suite

*   [Release 15.1: Billing and Revenue Management, Cloud Native Deployment, Elastic Charging Engine, Pricing Design Center, Billing Care, Business Operations Center](https://docs.oracle.com/en/industries/communications/billing-revenue/15.1/index.html)
    
*   [Release 15.0: Billing and Revenue Management, Cloud Native Deployment, Elastic Charging Engine, Pricing Design Center, Billing Care, Business Operations Center](https://docs.oracle.com/en/industries/communications/billing-revenue/15.0/index.html)

Oracle Session Delivery Management Cloud
========================================

Obtaining and installing the Oracle Session Delivery Management Cloud (OSDMC) service requires a multi-step process that includes tasks for you to perform in the Oracle Cloud and on premises. New customers must take steps to establish and set up their Oracle Cloud account before performing the OSDMC service installation procedures. See "Start Your Subscription" first. After you obtain your subscription, perform the "Top Tasks" in the order listed.

Get Started
-----------

### [Start Your Subscription](https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/paas/identity-cloud/index.html)

*   [Order Oracle Cloud applications](https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/get-started/subscriptions-cloud/csgsg/order-oracle-cloud-applications.html)
    
*   [Activate your order](https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/get-started/subscriptions-cloud/csgsg/activate-your-oracle-cloud-applications-order.html)
    
*   [Manage and monitor services](https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/get-started/subscriptions-cloud/manage-and-monitor-services.html)
    

### Top Tasks

*   [Establish an OSDMC Service Subscription](https://docs.oracle.com/en/industries/communications/session-delivery-management-cloud/gettingstarted/osdmc-deployment-process-and-procedures.html#GUID-2D5AE818-1F11-4DB7-8C9D-672FC442B707)
    
*   [Install and configure MCE](https://docs.oracle.com/en/industries/communications/session-delivery-management-cloud/gettingstarted/osdmc-deployment-process-and-procedures.html#GUID-95DC23BC-5F93-4F32-B8D5-87F14959886F)
    
*   [Upgrading the MCE](https://docs.oracle.com/en/industries/communications/session-delivery-management-cloud/gettingstarted/osdmc-deployment-process-and-procedures.html#GUID-13454561-C257-4DA7-B1E7-4B7BF66E9164)
    
*   [OSDMC Dashboard Page Display and Operations](https://docs.oracle.com/en/industries/communications/session-delivery-management-cloud/userguide/dashboard-manager.html#GUID-8BA5B233-C1E9-47F4-82D5-0C96BB4D8584)
    

### Other Tasks

*   [Device Manager](https://docs.oracle.com/en/industries/communications/session-delivery-management-cloud/userguide/device-manager.html#GUID-190A7E3C-5E03-47CD-8CAE-FD2DB8B8803F)

Oracle Communications Security Shield Cloud
===========================================

Obtaining and installing the Oracle Communications Security Shield Cloud (Security Shield) service requires a multi-step process that includes tasks for you to perform in the Oracle Cloud and on premises. New customers must establish and configure their Oracle Cloud Infrastructure account before performing the Security Shield service installation procedures. See "Start Your Subscription" first. After you obtain your subscription, perform the "Top Tasks" in the order listed.

Get Started
-----------

### Learn About Security Shield

*   [Security Shield Overview](https://docs.oracle.com/en/industries/communications/security-shield/userguide/security-shield-overview-orientation-topic.html#GUID-F34B9141-F7B2-474D-B9EE-254ED1F014C6)

Order and Service Management Documentation
==========================================

Oracle Communications Order and Service Management (OSM) is an order management system that coordinates the order fulfillment functions required to complete an order. OSM manages the actions required to fulfill the order across provisioning, shipping, inventory, billing, and other fulfillment systems.

*   [Release 7.5](https://docs.oracle.com/en/industries/communications/order-service-management/7.5/index.html)
    
*   [Release 7.4.1](https://docs.oracle.com/en/industries/communications/order-service-management/7.4.1/index.html)

Services Gatekeeper Documentation
=================================

Oracle Communications Services Gatekeeper is an API exposure platform that manages the entire API lifecycle. Use it to quickly and securely provide web resources using the provided APIs, or the APIs that you or your partners create. Services Gatekeeper is most useful for productizing the lightweight REST, SOAP, or other XML-based services used in today's world of web services.

*   [Release 7.0](https://docs.oracle.com/communications/E81149_01/index.htm)

Policy Administration
=====================

Oracle Insurance Policy Administration for Life and Annuity is an adaptive policy administration solution that enables insurers to accelerate time to market for differentiated insurance products across multiple lines of business, improve service to customers and sales channels throughout the insurance policy lifecycle, and reduce risk while optimizing business performance through highly flexible, rules-based policy administration.

Enterprise Session Router Documentation
=======================================

Oracle Enterprise Session Router is a next generation session routing proxy that incorporates both IETF SIP proxy and 3GPP Breakout Gateway Control Functions (BGCF) and is specifically design to control the routing of large volumes of SIP session signaling messages. The Enterprise Session Router provides high-performance SIP routing with scalable routing policies that increase overall network capacity and reduce costs. It plays a central role in Oracle's open session routing (OSR) architecture and helps service providers build a scalable, next-generation signaling core for SIP-based services.

*   [Release S-CZ10.0.0](https://docs.oracle.com/en/industries/communications/enterprise-session-router/10.0.0/index.html)
    
*   [Release S-CZ9.3.0](https://docs.oracle.com/en/industries/communications/enterprise-session-router/9.3.0/index.html)
    
*   [Release S-CZ9.2.0](https://docs.oracle.com/en/industries/communications/enterprise-session-router/9.2.0/index.html)
    
*   [Release S-CZ9.1.0](https://docs.oracle.com/en/industries/communications/enterprise-session-router/9.1.0/index.html)
    
*   [Release S-CZ9.0.0](https://docs.oracle.com/en/industries/communications/enterprise-session-router/9.0.0/index.html)

Converged Application Server Documentation
==========================================

Oracle Communications Converged Application Server is a carrier-class Java EE application server that supports SIP and a number of operational enhancements that allow it to meet the demanding requirements of next-generation Internet Protocol-based communications networks. The Converged Application Server implementation is based on Oracle's widely deployed and time-tested Java EE-compliant WebLogic Server product.

*   [Release 8.1](https://docs.oracle.com/en/industries/communications/converged-application-server/8.1/index.html)

PeopleSoft PeopleTools
======================

Online Help for PeopleTools
---------------------------

This page lists all documentation for the latest release. For earlier releases, select a release in the drop-down list.

Release 8.62 Release 8.61 Release 8.60 Release 8.59 Release 8.58 Release 8.57 Release 8.56

PeopleTools 8.62
----------------

### Online Help and PeopleBooks (8.62.01)

*   [Online Help (HTML)](https://www.oracle.com/pls/topic/lookup?ctx=en/applications/peoplesoft/peopletools&id=pt862help)
    
*   [PeopleBooks (PDF)](https://www.oracle.com/pls/topic/lookup?ctx=en/applications/peoplesoft/peopletools&id=pt862pb)
    

### Set Up Hosted Context-Sensitive Help

Set up your PeopleSoft applications to use this site for context-sensitive help. The hosted PeopleSoft Online Help can search multiple product families and releases and return context-sensitive help from all available resources.  
  
**Note:** If clicking a Help link on a PeopleSoft application page brought you to this page instead of to actual PeopleSoft online help content, it is likely that your PeopleSoft system is configured to access an older version of online help that is no longer available. You can access a current help library from this page and then search by the application page name. If you are a PeopleSoft system administrator, you can configure context-sensitive help to always display help for the latest application image or PeopleTools patch, so you'll never have to update your help configuration again.

*   [Configure Context-Sensitive Help Using the Hosted Online Help Website](https://www.oracle.com/pls/topic/lookup?ctx=psoft&id=ATPB_HOSTED)
    

### Install the Online Help Locally

If your organization has firewall restrictions that prevent you from using the hosted online help, you can install the help locally.

*   [Manage Locally Installed PeopleSoft Online Help](https://www.oracle.com/pls/topic/lookup?ctx=psoft&id=ATPB_LOCAL)
    

### Notices

You can access the latest version of all PeopleSoft documentation on this page. If you use links to older versions of the documentation that are no longer available, you'll be redirected to the latest version of the documentation or to this page.  
  
The Hosted Online Help and PeopleBook PDFs are updated with every PeopleSoft Update Image. The locally installable Online Help is updated and made available with the first PeopleSoft Update Image of each calendar year.  
  
Find regularly-posted announcements about new certifications, end-of-support or third-party retirement notices, and other platform-related information in [Tech Updates](https://support.oracle.com/epmos/faces/DocumentDisplay?id=764222.1)
.

PeopleTools 8.60
----------------

### Online Help and PeopleBooks (8.60.15)

*   [Online Help (HTML)](https://www.oracle.com/pls/topic/lookup?ctx=en/applications/peoplesoft/peopletools&id=pt860help)
    
*   [PeopleBooks (PDF)](https://www.oracle.com/pls/topic/lookup?ctx=en/applications/peoplesoft/peopletools&id=pt860pb)
    

### Documentation Updates

See [Documentation Updates](https://docs.oracle.com/cd/F33272_01/psft/pdf/PeopleTools%208.60.15%20Documentation%20Updates.pdf)
 for a cumulative list of updates to the online help and PeopleBooks through July 26, 2024.

### Set Up Hosted Context-Sensitive Help

Set up your PeopleSoft applications to use this site for context-sensitive help. The hosted PeopleSoft Online Help can search multiple product families and releases and return context-sensitive help from all available resources.  
  
**Note:** If clicking a Help link on a PeopleSoft application page brought you to this page instead of to actual PeopleSoft online help content, it is likely that your PeopleSoft system is configured to access an older version of online help that is no longer available. You can access a current help library from this page and then search by the application page name. If you are a PeopleSoft system administrator, you can configure context-sensitive help to always display help for the latest application image or PeopleTools patch, so you'll never have to update your help configuration again.

*   [Configure Context-Sensitive Help Using the Hosted Online Help Website](https://www.oracle.com/pls/topic/lookup?ctx=psoft&id=ATPB_HOSTED)
    

### Install the Online Help Locally

If your organization has firewall restrictions that prevent you from using the hosted online help, you can install the help locally.

*   [Manage Locally Installed PeopleSoft Online Help](https://www.oracle.com/pls/topic/lookup?ctx=psoft&id=ATPB_LOCAL)
    

### Notices

You can access the latest version of all PeopleSoft documentation on this page. If you use links to older versions of the documentation that are no longer available, you'll be redirected to the latest version of the documentation or to this page.  
  
The Hosted Online Help and PeopleBook PDFs are updated with every PeopleSoft Update Image. The locally installable Online Help is updated and made available with the first PeopleSoft Update Image of each calendar year.  
  
Find regularly-posted announcements about new certifications, end-of-support or third-party retirement notices, and other platform-related information in [Tech Updates](https://support.oracle.com/epmos/faces/DocumentDisplay?id=764222.1)
.

PeopleTools 8.56
----------------

### Online Help and PeopleBooks (8.56.12)

*   [Online Help (HTML)](https://www.oracle.com/pls/topic/lookup?ctx=psoft&id=pt856help)
    
*   [PeopleBooks (PDF)](https://www.oracle.com/pls/topic/lookup?ctx=psoft&id=pt856pb)
    

### Documentation Updates

See [Documentation Updates](https://docs.oracle.com/cd/F33272_01/psft/pdf/PeopleTools%208.56.12%20Documentation%20Updates.pdf)
 for a cumulative list of updates to the online help and PeopleBooks through October 12, 2018.

### Set Up Hosted Context-Sensitive Help

Set up your PeopleSoft applications to use this site for context-sensitive help. The hosted PeopleSoft Online Help can search multiple product families and releases and return context-sensitive help from all available resources.

*   [Configuring Context-Sensitive Help Using the Hosted Online Help Website](https://www.oracle.com/pls/topic/lookup?ctx=psoft&id=ATPB_HOSTED)
    

### Install the Online Help Locally

If your organization has firewall restrictions that prevent you from using the hosted online help, you can install the help locally.

*   [Manage Locally Installed PeopleSoft Online Help](https://docs.oracle.com/cd/E92519_02/pt856pbr3/eng/pt/atpb/task_IncludingOtherPeopleSoftOnlineHelpSitesInContext-SensitiveHelpLookups.html)
    

### Notices

You can access the latest version of all PeopleSoft documentation on this page. If you use links to older versions of the documentation that are no longer available, you'll be redirected to the latest version of the documentation or to this page.  
  
The Hosted Online Help and PeopleBook PDFs are updated with every PeopleSoft Update Image. The locally installable Online Help is updated and made available with the first PeopleSoft Update Image of each calendar year.  
  
Find regularly-posted announcements about new certifications, end-of-support or third-party retirement notices, and other platform-related information in [Tech Updates](https://support.oracle.com/epmos/faces/DocumentDisplay?id=764222.1)
.

Application Integration Architecture for Communications Documentation
=====================================================================

Oracle Application Integration Architecture (Oracle AIA) for Oracle Communications provides a common framework for integrating communications applications such as Siebel CRM, Oracle Communications Billing and Revenue Management, Oracle Communications Order and Service Management, Oracle Product Hub for Communications, and Oracle E-Business Suite.

*   [Release 12.3.3](https://docs.oracle.com/en/industries/communications/application-integration-architectures/12.3.3/index.html)
    
*   [Release 12.3.2](https://docs.oracle.com/en/industries/communications/application-integration-architectures/12.3.2/index.html)
    
*   [Release 12.3.1](https://docs.oracle.com/en/industries/communications/application-integration-architectures/12.3.1/index.html)
    
*   [Release 12.3](https://docs.oracle.com/en/industries/communications/application-integration-architectures/12.3/index.html)
    
*   [Release 12.2.1](https://docs.oracle.com/communications/F26886_01/index.htm)
    
*   [Release 12.2](https://docs.oracle.com/communications/E63483_01/index.htm)
    
*   [Release 11.6](https://docs.oracle.com/cd/E58077_01/index.htm)
    
*   [Release 11.5](https://docs.oracle.com/cd/E50268_01/index.htm)
    

Prior to Oracle AIA for Communications 12c, the pre-built integrations were packaged and installed separately from Oracle AIA Foundation Pack, along with their own documentation library. Release 12c packages and installs Oracle AIA Foundation Pack within the Communications pre-built integrations. Release 12.2 documentation includes information about installing and using the Communications pre-built integrations.

For information about general Oracle AIA concepts, see:

*   [Oracle Fusion Middleware Concepts and Technologies Guide for Oracle Application Integration Architecture Foundation Pack](https://docs.oracle.com/cd/E28280_01/doc.1111/e17363/toc.htm)
    

For developer information, see:

*   [Oracle Fusion Middleware Developerâs Guide for Oracle SOA Core Extension](https://docs.oracle.com/middleware/1213/soasuite/develop-sce/toc.htm)
    

[Back to main content](https://docs.oracle.com/en/industries/communications/application-integration-architectures/index.html#)

Unified Session Manager Documentation
=====================================

Oracle Communications Unified Session Manager combines an agile session core with the proven security, interoperability, reliability, and regulatory compliance capabilities of Oracle Communications Session Border Controller in a cost-effective, rapidly deployable product.

4.  [Interactive Session Recorder](https://docs.oracle.com/en/industries/communications/interactive-session-recorder/index.html)
    

Interactive Session Recorder Documentation
==========================================

Oracle Communications Interactive Session Recorder is a scalable and easy-to-deploy Internet Protocol (IP) communications session recording solution. Designed to overcome the connectivity, security, and control challenges associated with capturing real-time session recordings in IP telephony and unified communications (UC) environments, the solution is highly extensible with a rich feature set. Service providers and enterprises deploy it in compliance and quality assurance applications, and enterprise IT organizations use it to deliver private cloud-based session recording services.

*   [Release 6.4](https://docs.oracle.com/en/industries/communications/interactive-session-recorder/6.4/index.html)
    
*   [Release 6.3](https://docs.oracle.com/en/industries/communications/interactive-session-recorder/6.3/index.html)

Oracle Content Management
=========================

Oracle Content Management is a cloud-based content hub to drive omni-channel content management and accelerate experience delivery. It offers powerful collaboration, workflow management, and development capabilities to streamline the creation and delivery of content and improve customer and employee engagement.

Select an area to see important topics for you:

1.  [Home](https://docs.oracle.com/index.html)
    
2.  [Industries](https://docs.oracle.com/en/industries/index.html)
    
3.  [Communications](https://docs.oracle.com/en/industries/communications/index.html)
    
4.  [Tunneled Session Controller](https://docs.oracle.com/en/industries/communications/tunneled-session-controller/index.html)
    

Tunneled Session Controller SDK Documentation
=============================================

Oracle Communications Tunneled Session Management (TSM) is a feature on the Oracle Communications Session Border Controller. TSM improves firewall traversal for real-time communications and Over-the-Top (OTT) VoIP applications, reduces the dependency on SIP/TLS and SRTP by encrypting access-side VoIP within standardized tunnels. As calls or sessions traverse a TSM tunnel, the SBC will route all SIP and RTP traffic from within the TSM tunnel to the network core (or appropriate destination).

*   [Release 2.0.0](https://docs.oracle.com/cd/F22860_01/index.htm)
    
*   [Release 1.6.0](https://docs.oracle.com/cd/F22859_01/index.htm)
    
*   [Release 1.5.0](https://docs.oracle.com/cd/F22858_01/)
    

Note: The Tunneled Session Controller has been merged with Oracle Communications Session Border Controller starting with the S-CZ7.3.0 release. For more information, see the [Session Border Controller Documentation](http://docs.oracle.com/en/industries/communications/session-border-controller/)
. Use this SDK documentation in conjunction with the TSCF chapter of the _ACLI Configuration Guide_.

[Back to main content](https://docs.oracle.com/en/industries/communications/tunneled-session-controller/index.html#)

4.  [SD-WAN Aware](https://docs.oracle.com/en/industries/communications/sd-wan-aware/index.html)
    

Oracle SD-WAN Aware
===================

Oracle SD-WAN Aware is a centralized management system that easily configures, monitors, and provides extensive analytics for Oracle SD-WAN. Centralized control reduces the time and configuration errors associated with WAN management. Detailed performance data is correlated across the network. These capabilities deliver an easy-to-manage SD-WAN with unprecedented visibility into WAN and application performance.

Releases
--------

*   [Release 9.0](https://docs.oracle.com/en/industries/communications/sd-wan-aware/9.0/index.html)

*   [Release 8.0](https://docs.oracle.com/cd/F24715_01/index.htm)

JD Edwards EnterpriseOne Customer Relationship Management
=========================================================

Get Started
-----------

Oracle JD Edwards EnterpriseOne Customer Relationship Management (CRM) is a feature-rich solution that is embedded with other mission-critical processes, such as consensus-based forecasting, real-time order promising, case and service management, and lead and opportunity management. The Customer Relationship Management includes: Advanced Pricing, Case Management, Customer Self Service, Sales Order Management, and Service Management.

**Select your release:**

Release 9.2 Previous Releases

Session Monitor Documentation
=============================

Oracle Communications Session Monitor is a real-time, end-to-end service monitoring, troubleshooting, and analytics solution that provides a comprehensive view into VoIP and Unified Communications networks. You can use Session Monitor to monitor and troubleshoot call quality issues, prevent fraud, and minimize subscriber churn.

Enterprise Communications Broker Documentation
==============================================

Oracle Enterprise Communications Broker is a core communications controller purpose-built to simplify complex, multivendor Unified Communications (UC) networks and extend services to mobile users. It abstracts key communications services and centralizes session management, providing a vendor-neutral infrastructure for delivery of next generation UC applications.

*   [Release P-CZ5.0.0](https://docs.oracle.com/en/industries/communications/enterprise-communications-broker/5.0.0/index.html)

Health
======

Connected technologies and unified data empower individuals and enable the health ecosystem to accelerate innovation and influence health outcomes. Oracle Health is building an open healthcare platform with intelligent tools for data-driven, human-centric healthcare experiences to connect consumers, healthcare providers, payers, and public health and life sciences organizations.  
  
**Want to learn more?** Join the conversation in the [Oracle Health Community](https://community.oracle.com/oraclehealth/)
  
  
**Need help right now?** Contact [Oracle Health Support Services](https://www.oracle.com/health/services/support/)
 or [Healthcare Support](https://www.oracle.com/industries/health-sciences/support.html)

Network Charging and Control Documentation
==========================================

Oracle Communications Network Charging and Control (NCC) applications enable communications service providers to control telephony sessions and events, to integrate network elements with online charging systems, and to provide voucher based recharge services. Use NCC to manage telephony and messaging services, define rules for telephony session and event control, provide device based self-care, and to integrate with charging systems for online authorization of sessions and events.

*   [Release 15.1](https://docs.oracle.com/communications/G13327_01/index.htm)

Subscriber-Aware Load Balancer Documentation
============================================

Oracle Communications Subscriber-Aware Load Balancer enables linear, non-disruptive scaling of capacity to two million subscribers from a single Session Initiation Protocol (SIP) Internet Protocol (IP) address. It supports the delivery of any IP Multimedia Subsystem (IMS), Rich Communications Services (RCS), or next-generation network (NGN) service as well as any SIP application -- voice, video, presence, messaging, and multimedia -- over any mobile or fixed line access network, including the internet.

*   [Release S-CZ10.0.0](https://docs.oracle.com/en/industries/communications/subscriber-aware-load-balancer/10.0.0/index.html)

4.  [SD-WAN Edge](https://docs.oracle.com/en/industries/communications/sd-wan-edge/index.html)
    

Oracle SD-WAN Edge
==================

Oracle SD-WAN Edge solutions provide key network edge services critical to branch office simplification and simplified WAN management, including SD-WAN, routing, firewall, and WAN optimization, and offer flexible deployment options at the physical edge, the virtual edge, or in the cloud. Oracle SD-WAN enables enterprises to transform their traditional WAN, augmenting or replacing MPLS with high-bandwidth, inexpensive internet connections, and delivering a network that is easy to deploy and manage, but without requiring forklift upgrades. Thanks to patented technology that does continuous unidirectional measurement of all paths and offers subsecond response to not just link failures but also congestion-based brownouts, Oracle failsafe SD-WAN delivers better-than-MPLS high availability and exceptional QoE for applications site-to-site and site-to-cloud.

Releases
--------

Enterprise Session Border Controller Documentation
==================================================

Oracle Enterprise Session Border Controller (E-SBC) connects disparate Internet Protocol (IP) communications networks while mitigating security threats, curing interoperability problems, and ensuring reliable communications. It protects and controls real-time voice, video, and Unified Communications (UC) as they traverse IP network borders.

4.  [Session Router](https://docs.oracle.com/en/industries/communications/session-router/index.html)
    

Session Router Documentation
============================

Oracle Communications Session Router is a next generation session routing proxy that incorporates both IETF SIP proxy and 3GPP Breakout Gateway Control Functions (BGCF) and is specifically design to control the routing of large volumes of SIP session signaling messages. Session Router provides high-performance SIP routing with scalable routing policies that increase overall network capacity and reduce costs. It plays a central role in Oracle's open session routing (OSR) architecture and helps service providers build a scalable, next-generation signaling core for SIP-based services.

4.  [Session Border Controller](https://docs.oracle.com/en/industries/communications/session-border-controller/index.html)
    

Session Border Controller Documentation
=======================================

Oracle Communications Session Border Controller is the industry-leading session border controller (SBC) for fixed line, mobile, and over-the-top (OTT) services. Based on Acme Packet OS, Oracle Communications Session Border Controller operates on Oracle's range of purpose-built hardware platforms or general-purpose servers to deliver a unique combination of performance, capacity, high availability, and manageability that has made it the most widely deployed SBC in the world.

The functions offered by Oracle Communications Session Border Controller satisfy critical service provider requirements in five major areas: security, interoperability, reliability and quality, regulatory compliance, and revenue/cost optimization.

--------
